description: Keep fixtures minimal but realistic enough to exercise the public behavior under test.
body: Use this when a test needs files, database rows, API payloads, DOM state, clock values, or generated artifacts. The fixture should include only fields required for the behavior while preserving invariants that production code assumes, such as IDs, timestamps, parent-child links, or encoding. Do not copy large production samples, secret-bearing data, or broad snapshots when a targeted fixture explains intent. Execution action: name the fixture around the behavior, keep unrelated values neutral, and assert the observable output. Verification signals include readable fixture setup, absence of hidden global state, deterministic cleanup, and failing output that points to the behavior rather than fixture noise. Use when Code Testing work touches test runner output, fixture intent, coverage boundaries, flake history, and negative paths. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include focused test output, regression tests, fixture assertions, and rerun evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves testing implementation details only, masking flakes, and claiming coverage without behavior proof, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
